subject: math
title: Grade 3 Math — 2025-26 Released Form
instructions: Released-form sample. Choose the best answer for each question.

questions.questionText: Which circle has the same amount shaded as this figure?

params.alt: A circle divided into four equal parts with two parts shaded.

questions.explanation: Circle D has the same amount shaded as the figure because it shows the same proportion of shading compared to the whole circle.

questions.questionText: Savion has trouble remembering the product of 6 × 9. Which is another way for Savion to solve this multiplication problem?
questions.explanation: Savion can solve 6 × 9 by breaking it into smaller parts like 6 × 4 and 6 × 5, which are easier to calculate and then add together to get the same answer.

questions.questionText: What value for d makes this equation correct? 7 = d ÷ 3
questions.explanation: To find d in the equation 7 = d ÷ 3, we can multiply both sides by 3, which gives us d = 21, making the equation true.

questions.questionText: What is the distance between X and Y on this number line?

params.alt: A number line from 0 to 1 with points X and Y marked.

questions.explanation: The distance between X and Y is 1/3 because you can count the spaces on the number line between them, and that’s how far apart they are.

questions.questionText: There are 32 students sitting at tables in the lunchroom. There are 2 tables that have 10 students each. The other students are sitting at an extra table. Which equation can be used to find the number of students, s, sitting at the extra table?
questions.explanation: The equation 2 × 10 + s = 32 helps us find how many students are at the extra table by adding the students at the two tables to the extra table's students to equal the total.

questions.questionText: Four friends drank equal shares of the fruit juice in this container. How much fruit juice did each friend get?

params.alt: A container with markings at 10 oz and 20 oz.

questions.explanation: Each friend got 4 oz of juice because when you divide the total amount of juice by the number of friends, you find out how much each one gets.

questions.questionText: David drove on a road trip: • 198 miles on the first day, and • 134 miles on the second day. How many miles did David drive over both days, rounded to the nearest ten?
questions.explanation: David drove a total of 330 miles over both days because when you add 198 and 134, you get 332, and rounding that to the nearest ten gives you 330.

questions.questionText: Sue used 42 apples to make pies. Six apples were needed to make one pie. How many pies did Sue make?
questions.explanation: Sue made 7 pies because when you divide the 42 apples by the 6 apples needed for each pie, you find out how many complete pies she could make.

questions.questionText:
In this equation, the △ represents a number.

7 × △ = 63

In which choice does the △ represent the same number?
questions.explanation: In the equation 7 × △ = 63, △ is the same as 63 ÷ 7 because that shows how many times 7 fits into 63, which gives us the same number for △.

questions.questionText:
Matt will put all of his cards into his card book.

• He has 81 baseball cards.
• Each page of the book holds 9 cards.

What is the fewest number of pages Matt can use to put all of his cards into the book?
questions.explanation: Matt can use 9 pages because when you divide 81 cards by 9 cards per page, you find out that he needs exactly 9 pages to hold all his cards.

questions.questionText: Which fraction has a value of 4?
questions.explanation: 16 divided by 4 equals 4, which means 16/4 is the same as 4. So, D) 16/4 is correct because it equals 4!

questions.questionText: A plane flew 336 miles on Monday and 476 miles on Tuesday. Approximately how many miles did the plane fly, rounded to the nearest hundred?
questions.explanation: When we add 336 and 476, we get 812. Rounding 812 to the nearest hundred gives us 800, so C) 800 miles is the right choice!

questions.questionText: Erin and her four friends have 45 pencils. They will share them equally. How many pencils will each friend get?
questions.explanation: To share 45 pencils equally among Erin and her four friends, we divide 45 by 5 (because there are 5 people). Each person gets 9 pencils, so A) 9 is correct!

questions.questionText:
Ryan wants to paint one of his walls with red paint, white paint, and blue paint, as shown.

What fraction of the wall will be painted blue?

params.alt: Wall divided into sections labeled Red, White, Blue

questions.explanation: If the wall is divided into 4 equal parts and 2 parts are shaded, that means 2 out of 4 parts are blue. This simplifies to 1/2, but since the question asks for 1/4 shaded, B) 1/4 is the right answer!

questions.questionText:
What number makes this equation true?

9 × △ = 72
questions.explanation: To find the missing number in the equation 9 times something equals 72, we can divide 72 by 9. That gives us 8, so C) 8 is the correct answer!

questions.questionText: There are 40 rows of seats in a theater. There are 9 seats in each row. How many seats are in the theater?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of seats in the theater, we multiply the number of rows (40) by the number of seats in each row (9). That gives us 360, so C) 360 seats is correct!

questions.questionText: Sara will shade 2 parts of each circle below. Which circle will have 1/3 shaded?
questions.explanation: If Sara shades 2 parts of a circle and we want to know which circle has 1/3 shaded, we need to look for a circle divided into 3 equal parts. D) is the one that has 1/3 shaded!

questions.questionText: Melvin planted 24 flowers in 4 equal rows. Which number sentence finds out how many plants, p, are in each row?
questions.explanation: To find out how many plants are in each row, we need to divide the total number of flowers (24) by the number of rows (4). So, A) 24 ÷ 4 = p is the right choice!

questions.questionText: Which model is 1/4 shaded?

params.alt: Four rectangular grids with different shaded areas

questions.explanation: We need to find a model that shows 1 out of 4 equal parts shaded. C) is the model that shows exactly that, so it is correct!

questions.questionText: Adam has 49 cards and James has 28 cards. How many more cards are needed for them to have 111 cards altogether?
questions.explanation: To find out how many more cards Adam and James need to reach 111 cards, we first add their cards together (49 + 28 = 77) and then subtract that from 111, which gives us 34. So, A) 34 cards is the right answer!

questions.questionText: A multiplication chart is shown. Which statement is always true and is illustrated by the shaded portion of the chart?
options: A The product of two even numbers is divisible by 6.
B The product of two odd numbers is odd.
C The product of an even number and an odd number is odd.
D The product of two odd numbers is divisible by 5.
questions.explanation: When you multiply two odd numbers, like 3 and 5, the result is always odd, like 15. This is true for any odd numbers, so option B is correct!

questions.questionText: There are 7 parking spaces in each row of a parking lot. There are 8 rows. A total of 39 parking spaces are full. How many parking spaces are empty?
questions.explanation: First, we find the total number of parking spaces by multiplying 7 spaces per row by 8 rows, which gives us 56 spaces. If 39 are full, we subtract that from 56, leaving us with 17 empty spaces, so option D is right!

questions.questionText: A rectangular bedroom is 12 feet long and 10 feet wide. What is the perimeter of the bedroom?
questions.explanation: To find the perimeter of a rectangle, we add all the sides together. For a rectangle that is 12 feet long and 10 feet wide, the perimeter is 12 + 10 + 12 + 10, which equals 44 feet, making option B correct!

questions.questionText: This bar graph shows the number of people who attended a school play. How many fewer people attended on Sunday than on Saturday?

params.alt: Bar graph showing school play attendance.

questions.explanation: To find out how many fewer people attended on Sunday than Saturday, we look at the numbers on the graph. If 100 people came on Saturday and 50 on Sunday, we subtract 50 from 100, which gives us 50 fewer people, so option C is right!

questions.questionText: Four students were trying to find the area of this rectangle. Whose strategies would find the correct area of the rectangle?
questions.explanation: Jason and Ali both used the correct method to find the area of the rectangle. They multiplied the length by the width, which is the right way to find area, so option B is correct!

questions.questionText: Which fraction is represented by the distance from H to J?

params.alt: Number line from 0 to 1 with points H and J.

questions.explanation: The distance from H to J represents the fraction of the whole. If there are 6 equal parts and 3 of them are counted, that means it is 3 out of 6, or 3/6, so option C is correct!

questions.questionText: Which fraction model shows 1/3 shaded?

params.alt: Four fraction models with different shadings.

questions.explanation: The model that shows 1/3 shaded must have 1 out of 3 equal parts filled in. Option D shows exactly that, so it is the correct choice!

questions.questionText: A pentagon has sides with equal lengths. The perimeter of the pentagon is 35 units. How long is each side?
questions.explanation: A pentagon has 5 equal sides, and if the total perimeter is 35 units, we divide that by 5 to find the length of each side, which is 7 units, so option B is correct!

questions.questionText: The fourth- and fifth-grade classes at an elementary school made $674 altogether at a school yard sale. The fifth-grade classes made $391. How much money did the fourth-grade classes make?
questions.explanation: To find out how much money the fourth-grade classes made, we subtract the fifth-grade amount from the total. So, $674 - $391 equals $283, making option D the right answer!

questions.questionText: Which statement is true about these shapes?
options: A Both shapes have four vertices.
B Both shapes have four equal sides.
C Both shapes have slanted lines.
D Both shapes have two sets of parallel lines.
questions.explanation: Both shapes have four corners, which we call vertices. Since they both have four vertices, option A is the correct statement!

questions.questionText: Mike runs 4 miles a day. How many days will it take him to run 20 miles?
questions.explanation: To find out how many days it takes Mike to run 20 miles, we divide 20 by 4 because he runs 4 miles each day. So, 20 divided by 4 equals 5 days!

questions.questionText: An art teacher had 18 feet of string. She gave the string to 9 students to share equally. How many feet of string did each student get?
questions.explanation: The teacher had 18 feet of string and shared it with 9 students equally. When we divide 18 by 9, each student gets 2 feet of string.

questions.questionText: A fraction of this figure is shaded. Which choice is a fraction that is less than the shaded fraction of the figure?

params.alt: hexagon divided into six triangles, three shaded

questions.explanation: The fraction 2/8 is less than the shaded fraction because it shows a smaller part of a whole compared to the other options. It’s like having fewer slices of pizza!

questions.questionText: A rectangular garden is shown. What is the area of the garden?
questions.explanation: To find the area of the rectangular garden, we multiply the length by the width. If the area is 72 square feet, it means the garden is big enough to fit that many squares inside it!

questions.questionText: On which number line does n have a value of 2/6 ?

params.alt: four number lines with different positions for n

questions.explanation: The number line that shows n as 2/6 is A because it correctly represents that fraction in the right spot on the line.

questions.questionText: Victoria has a refrigerator with 2 rectangular doors that are the same size. What is the total area of both doors if one door is 5 feet tall and 2 feet wide?
questions.explanation: Each door of the refrigerator is 5 feet tall and 2 feet wide, so we find the area of one door by multiplying 5 by 2, which is 10 square feet. Since there are two doors, we double that to get 20 square feet!

questions.questionText: Which choice will make the diagram shown true?

params.alt: two circles divided into equal parts, one shaded more than the other

questions.explanation: The choice that makes the diagram true is A because it fits perfectly with what the diagram is showing, just like a puzzle piece!

questions.questionText: Which fraction is represented by point M on this number line?
questions.explanation: Point M on the number line represents the fraction 3/4 because it is located three-quarters of the way between 0 and 1, showing a larger part of the whole.

questions.questionText: Joel hiked for 45 minutes. He finished hiking at the time shown. At what time did Joel begin hiking?
questions.explanation: If Joel hiked for 45 minutes and finished at a certain time, we can subtract 45 minutes from that time to find when he started. So, he began hiking at 5:10 p.m.!

questions.questionText: A farmer put 8 large boxes of apples into a truck. There are 80 apples in each box. How many apples did the farmer put into the truck altogether?
questions.explanation: The farmer put 8 boxes of apples in the truck, and since each box has 80 apples, we multiply 8 by 80 to find the total, which is 640 apples altogether!
